title: K-PLUG: KNOWLEDGE-INJECTED PRE-TRAINED LANGUAGE MODEL FOR NATURAL LANGUAGE UNDERSTANDING AND GENERATION

structured.summary: This paper proposes K-PLUG, a knowledge-injected pre-trained language model tailored for e-commerce tasks that integrates four types of domain knowledge into both encoder and decoder via five novel pre-training objectives. It demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on knowledge base completion, abstractive summarization, and multi-turn dialogue tasks within the Chinese e-commerce domain.

strengths.point: Integration of multiple e-commerce knowledge domains into pre-training
strengths.evidence: The model explicitly incorporates knowledge bases, product aspects, categories, and unique selling propositions through five novel objectives (KMLM, KMS2S, PEABD, PECC, PEASG).

strengths.point: Strong empirical results across diverse downstream tasks
strengths.evidence: K-PLUG achieves SOTA on product KB completion, abstractive summarization (ROUGE improvements), and multi-turn dialogue tasks, outperforming text-only and some multimodal baselines.

strengths.point: Comprehensive ablation studies and human evaluation
strengths.evidence: Ablations show the necessity of each objective and domain data; human evaluation confirms better knowledge utilization and output quality.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient comparison with recent knowledge-injection PLMs
weaknesses.evidence: The paper lacks thorough discussion and experimental comparison with concurrent methods like ERNIE-GEN or KEPLER that also inject knowledge into encoder-decoder models.
Motivation for choosing the specific five objectives and four knowledge domains is limited; details on how knowledge tokens are identified and how PEASG aligns inputs and outputs are vague.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation limitations regarding baselines and metrics
weaknesses.evidence: Baselines are not fully balanced in training data scale and architecture; reliance on ROUGE alone for generation metrics is insufficient especially for factual correctness; dialogue dataset and setup details are under-explained.
Key hyperparameters, mask proportions, optimizer settings, and pre-training corpus cleaning/details are not fully disclosed.

weaknesses.point: Limited discussion on generalizability and practical deployment
weaknesses.evidence: No analysis on applicability beyond Chinese e-commerce domain, handling of noisy/conflicting knowledge, or impact on model size and inference efficiency.

questions.question: How are knowledge tokens identified precisely for masking in KMLM and KMS2S?
questions.motivation: Accurate identification of knowledge tokens is critical for effective knowledge injection and reproducibility.

questions.question: How do the KMLM and KMS2S objectives complement or overlap, and what is their individual contribution?
questions.motivation: Understanding redundancy or synergy between these similar masked objectives informs objective design and model efficiency.

questions.question: Can the knowledge-injection framework be generalized to other domains or languages?
questions.motivation: Assessing generalizability determines the broader impact and applicability of the proposed approach.

critique_points.point_001: Insufficient comparison with recent knowledge-injection PLMs. The paper lacks thorough discussion and experimental comparison with concurrent methods like ERNIE-GEN or KEPLER that also inject knowledge into encoder-decoder models
critique_points.point_002: Methodological clarity and justification issues. Motivation for choosing the specific five objectives and four knowledge domains is limited; details on how knowledge tokens are identified and how PEASG aligns inputs and outputs are vague
critique_points.point_003: Evaluation limitations regarding baselines and metrics. Baselines are not fully balanced in training data scale and architecture; reliance on ROUGE alone for generation metrics is insufficient especially for factual correctness; dialogue dataset and setup details are under-explained
critique_points.point_004: Reproducibility concerns. Key hyperparameters, mask proportions, optimizer settings, and pre-training corpus cleaning/details are not fully disclosed
critique_points.point_005: Limited discussion on generalizability and practical deployment. No analysis on applicability beyond Chinese e-commerce domain, handling of noisy/conflicting knowledge, or impact on model size and inference efficiency
